items.headline: MCP tool timeout now configurable, CLI auth lands
items.body: 2.1.187 fixes remote MCP tool calls that hang past five minutes — they now abort with an error, configurable via CLAUDE_CODE_MCP_TOOL_IDLE_TIMEOUT. 2.1.186 adds `claude mcp login/logout <name>` to handle MCP auth from the CLI without the interactive menu. The timeout fix is directly relevant for ADO MCP Server: Azure DevOps API calls under load can stall, and a configurable abort beats a silent hang.

items.headline: Anthropic productizes the ADO MCP pattern
items.body: Claude Tag puts Claude in Slack channels with scoped tool access, multi-user context, and ambient monitoring — structurally the same thing ADO MCP does inside Azure DevOps. Anthropic's design choices are worth examining: per-channel permission scoping, token spend limits, and full audit logs. Those three controls are the production-grade version of what any workflow-embedded MCP server eventually needs.
